passage.title: Morning Routine Check
passage.text: Our home tested two ways to follow the after-school routine last month. Sia used one long spoken reminder, but Noah used picture routine cards. After one week, Noah finished sooner because the pictures showed the order clearly. My mom timed each routine and wrote the minutes on a chart. When Sia saw the difference, she changed to picture routine cards. Two days later, she also finished her routine with fewer reminders. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that clear pictures can make a routine easier to follow.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Learning a clearer way to follow a home routine
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: What did Noah use to follow the routine?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: The way two things are not the same
A loud sound in a room
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does routine mean in the first sentence?
choices: A person who visits a club
A set of steps done in the usual order
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Sia switch to picture routine cards?
choices: The group wanted to decorate the room more
The first tool was missing from the class
She thought the pictures would help her see each step clearly
The teacher told the student to stop working
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
